title: Dry Bulk Shipping - July 17, 2018
summary: Dry bulk rates break out of recent ranges - Capesize rates have reached 25,000/d, clearly breaking out of Atlantic Capesize cargo flow peaking for now - We believe the recent strong activity of cargo flow out of August is historically the turning point - Historically, the second half of August has seen considerable Futures curve now flattish to spot across all segments - The Freight Futures market is now trading Fleet growth for the year now tracks at ~2% - With extremely low scrapping and deliveries in line with Short term outlook cautious - We expect a pause in the recent rally, as strong fixing in July should lead to Long term outlook neutral - Although the fundamental drivers for dry bulk look promising, the macro
